questions.question: In which part of the respiratory system, gaseous exchange takes place?
___________ is located between two pleural sacs and is the central compartment of the thoracic cavity?
Which of the following statements is true about involuntary breathing?
options: It is controlled by the bronchioles
It is controlled by the pulmonary arterioles
It is controlled by the alveolar-capillary network
It is controlled by the neurons, located in the medulla and pons

questions.question: Which of the following are parts of the human respiratory system?
Which of the following gas is released out during the process of respiration?
The tiny air sacs present in human lungs is called_______.
Which of the following functions by filtering and keeping the mucus and dirt away from our lungs?
The total number of alveoli present in the human lungs is estimated to be around ______.
The exchange of gases between the external environment and the lungs______.
Which one of the following statements is false about the trachea?
options: It splits into the right and left lungs

questions.question: The maximum volume of air contained in the lung by a full forced inhalation is called _________.
Which one of the following is correct regarding larynx?
options: It prevents the invading pathogens into the trachea
It is an organ made of cartilage and connects the pharynx to the trachea

questions.question: Which of the following is the function of the trachea?
options: Exhales the air from the body

questions.question: Which of these statements is true about internal respiration?
options: Exchange of gases between the bloodstream and tissue cells
Exchange of gases between alveoli and the bloodstream
Breathing between the atmosphere and the alveoli

questions.question: Which of the following organs functions as an air conditioner?
The normal breathing process is controlled by ____________.
In Aves, the exchange of gases occurs within the __________.
Which of the following statements is true about the entry of air into the lungs?
options: Air enters the body and travels to the lungs through the mouth and the nose
Air enters the body and travels to the lungs through the oesophagus and gullet
Air enters the body and travels to the lungs through the windpipe and the pores
Air enters the body and travels to the lungs through the nose and the nervous system

questions.question: The windpipe is also called the ________.
In Earthworms, the process of respiration is through ________.
